Title: US-Iran talks not a definitive fix for global energy market uncertainty, says Akmal
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Recent talks between the United States and Iran should not be viewed as a definitive solution to ongoing uncertainties in the global energy market, Economy Minister Datuk Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said.
He said developments in the Middle East continue to warrant close attention as the geopolitical situation remains fluid and could still affect global oil supplies.
Akmal noted that Brent crude prices had fallen and remained below US$90 a barrel last week, while spot oil prices stayed under US$100 a barrel.
"This indicates that developments over the past week have had a positive effect on oil prices.
"However, it is premature to conclude that the issue has been resolved, as we are still awaiting further developments in the Middle East.
"While oil prices are expected to ease slightly further this week, the situation remains volatile," he told reporters after attending the Johor Baru parliamentary constituency's Perdana Aidiladha korban programme at Masjid Jamek Bandar Baru Uda today.
Akmal said the key concern now is ensuring the continued flow of physical crude oil supplies into the global market, particularly as the Strait of Hormuz, one of the world's most important energy shipping routes, has yet to be fully reopened.
Any disruption along the route, he said, could affect global energy supply chains and destabilise oil prices.
"The government will continue coordinating the necessary intervention measures to mitigate the impact of market uncertainty until the situation stabilises," he said.
Media reports previously stated that negotiators from the United States and Iran had reached a preliminary agreement to extend a 60-day ceasefire and establish a framework for further nuclear negotiations.
The memorandum of understanding is still subject to final approval by US President Donald Trump, although negotiators from both sides have reportedly finalised most of the agreement's terms.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'True heroine': Senator praises USM graduate for viral act of cross-cultural kindness
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: A young woman has been praised for quietly helping a needy man buy food and drinks at a cafe, in an act described as reflecting the compassion and unity that continue to exist in Malaysian society.
Senator Dr R. A. Lingeshwaran shared the incident on social media, saying he witnessed the encounter at a Coffee Bean outlet here this morning.
He said an underprivileged Chinese man had been approaching patrons and seeking assistance.
"Many people noticed him, but what happened next made me smile and feel proud to be Malaysian," he said in a Facebook post.
Lingeshwaran said a young Malay woman, identified as Siti Zulaiqah, a graduate of Universiti Sains Malaysia, stepped forward to help.
Without hesitation, she accompanied the man to the counter and invited him to choose the food and drinks he wanted before paying for the items, he said.
"There were no cameras, no publicity and no intention of seeking praise. It was simply a small act born from a big heart," he added.
Describing Siti as an example of kindness in action, Lingeshwaran said meaningful acts did not require wealth, power or status.
"Sometimes, a small act carried out with sincerity can mean so much to someone in need," he said.
He said Siti deserved recognition for the values she had displayed.
"She is proof that kindness, compassion and unity are still alive in our society," he said.
Lingeshwaran added that the incident reflected the Malaysia many people hoped to see, where individuals help one another regardless of race, religion or background.
He also congratulated Siti, describing her as an inspiration to fellow Malaysians.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Man arrested after viral road rage incident in Bayan Lepas
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: Police have arrested a 37-year-old man following a road rage incident where he allegedly criminally intimidated another driver and made obscene gestures along Jalan Tun Dr Awang on Thursday.
Southwest district police chief Assistant Commissioner Anuwal Ab Wahab said the incident occurred at 11.27am when the suspect, driving a Perodua Myvi, abruptly cut in front of the complainant's vehicle before stopping.
He said the suspect then stepped out of his car, wielding an object believed to be a metal rod and swung it towards the complainant's car,
"The incident was recorded by the complainant's dashboard camera, and the video later went viral on social media, drawing public attention," he said in a statement today.
Anuwal said police arrested the suspect following investigations into the incident.
"Checks revealed that the man has previous criminal and drug-related records, while a preliminary urine screening test found him positive for methamphetamine," he added.
Police also seized a Perodua Myvi, a PVC pipe, vehicle keys and clothing, believed to have been used during the incident to facilitate investigations.
The suspect was brought before the Balik Pulau magistrate's court and has been remanded for three days until May 31.
The case is being investigated under Section 506 of the Penal Code for criminal intimidation.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Devotees flock to Penang temples ahead of Wesak as grand floral float nears completion
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: Thousands of devotees have flocked to Buddhist temples across Penang ahead of Wesak Day tomorrow, as final touches are being made to the state's main procession float.
At the Malaysian Buddhist Association (MBA) here, where the towering 4.5m-tall float is being assembled, worshippers streamed in throughout the day while volunteers from the Penang Ohara Floral Art Society worked amid piles of freshly delivered flowers, transforming the structure into a vibrant sea of colours ahead of Sunday's state-level celebrations.
Devotees lit joss sticks and offered prayers inside the temple, while outside, teams of volunteers carefully arranged thousands of flowers that will adorn the float when it takes to the streets of George Town.
Floral arrangement adviser Chin Lee Siew, 80, said preparations began on Thursday and intensified after flowers started arriving two days ago.
The float, built on a white base, is now about 90 per cent complete.
"We received a very large number of flower donations this year. Some were worth thousands of ringgit. The response has been overwhelming," he told the New Straits Times.
As workers sorted blooms by colour and type, boxes of flowers continued arriving at the temple grounds, adding to an already impressive collection of chrysanthemums, orchids, carnations and other floral varieties.
The abundance of donations has been so significant that organisers have more flowers than they can use.
"There will be flowers left over after we complete the float. We will share them with other participating floats and donate the remainder to Buddhist temples.
"It shows that the spirit of giving remains strong. People are still contributing generously and supporting Wesak celebrations," Chin added.
The floral masterpiece will eventually be illuminated by LED lights, creating a striking visual spectacle when it joins 22 other floats in the annual Wesak procession.
For volunteer Teoh Yeam Chuan, 45, the preparations represented far more than decorative work.
Having visited the temple since childhood, he regarded the annual celebration as an enduring symbol of unity and goodwill.
"I've been coming here since I was young. Every year, the atmosphere is special because people gather not only to celebrate Wesak but also to pray for peace, happiness and harmony," he said.
Teoh said the procession has long served as a bridge between temples and the wider community.
"The floats are beautiful, but their purpose is deeper than that. They bring blessings to the people and inspire others to visit temples, reflect on life and do good deeds.
"It doesn't matter what race or religion you are. The message is universal — harmony, kindness and compassion for everyone," he said.
He said that before the age of social media and smartphones, the annual procession allowed people to experience Buddhist traditions and teachings firsthand.
"Today, it continues to serve as a source of inspiration. Someone watching the procession may feel encouraged to visit a temple, pray or simply become a better person. That's the spirit behind it," he added.
Among those visiting the temple ahead of Wesak Day was businessman Datuk Ang Thean Hock, 73, who has been attending prayers there for about 20 years.
"I come every year to pray for happiness, good health and prosperity.
"Wesak is a meaningful occasion because it reminds us to appreciate what we have and to do good for others," he said.
Preparations for the state-level celebration have been underway for the past two weeks.
This year's celebrations, themed "Bridging Through Digital, Cultivating Wisdom Together", will feature 23 floats from Buddhist temples and organisations across the state.
The oil lamp-lighting ceremony will take place this evening, while the grand procession will begin at 6pm tomorrow.
Wesak Day is the most sacred festival for Buddhists around the world as it commemorates three important events in the life of Siddhartha Gautama, who later became the Buddha: his birth as a prince in Lumbini, his attainment of enlightenment at the age of 35, and his passing into Nirvana at the age of 80.
The celebration takes place on the full moon of the ancient lunar month of Vesakha, which usually falls in May.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: US warns capable of resuming war with Iran as deal remains elusive
Article:
WASHINGTON: The United States warned on Saturday it was "more than capable" of resuming war with Iran after President Donald Trump said any peace deal must adhere to his red lines, including Teheran never being able to develop nuclear weapons.
The White House had signalled Trump was close to a decision on an initial deal after weeks of mixed signals in tenuous negotiations, though Teheran denied there was a final agreement on ending the Middle East conflict that has jolted the global economy.
US sources had told AFP the deal was waiting on Trump's sign-off, but the president had made no decision after a two-hour meeting in the White House Situation Room on Friday in Washington.
Meanwhile, Pentagon chief Pete Hegseth, while attending a major Asia defence summit in Singapore, said on Saturday local time that Washington could restart the war if it wanted.
"Our ability to recommence if necessary is (that) we are more than capable, our stockpiles are more than suited for that, both there and around the globe because of how we balance exquisite and more plentiful munitions," he said.
That echoed the US Central Comand (CENTCOM), which posted on X that American forces "remain present and vigilant across the region."
The efforts to strike a deal mediated by Pakistan, were thrown into question this week by US strikes on the southern Iranian port of Bandar Abbas countered by retaliatory fire from Iran.
Nevertheless diplomacy continued including in a parallel process to stop fighting in Lebanon - which Iran has insisted be included in any formal end to the war and where Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu announced Israeli forces had advanced further even as military delegations from both nations met at the Pentagon in Washington.
Trump's priorities in any deal included Iran agreeing to never developing nuclear weapons and the re-opening of the blockaded Strait of Hormuz maritime route, he said in a social media post in which he also announced the Situation Room meeting.
"President Trump will only make a deal that is good for America and satisfies his red lines," a White House official told AFP after the meeting.
"Iran can never possess a nuclear weapon," the official added.
COMPETING CONDITIONS
Iranian foreign ministry spokesman Esmaeil Baqaei pushed back on Trump's conditions, telling state media that the Islamic republic "said goodbye to the language of 'must' 47 years ago."
Exchanges of messages were continuing, he added, but "no final agreement has been reached yet."
In a phone call with the Emir of Qatar, Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian said Iran was ready to achieve a "dignified framework" to end the war, according to state news agency IRNA.
In his post, Trump said Tehran would remove mines from the Strait of Hormuz and end its closure of the waterway with "no tolls," while the US would lift its parallel blockade of Iranian ports.
The two countries would also coordinate on removing and destroying Iran's enriched uranium, he said, adding that "no money will be exchanged, until further notice."
Iran's Fars news agency, however, cited sources as saying Tehran was demanding "the immediate release of US$12 billion in frozen Iranian assets" before moving to the next phase of negotiations.
On the toll-free reopening of Hormuz, the sources said "no such clause appears in the text of the agreement," while Trump's comment on destroying Iran's nuclear material "is fundamentally baseless."
Ali, a resident of the city of Tonekabon north of Tehran, said there would likely be more strife to come.
"Both sides are speaking in a way that keeps their supporters satisfied. It's not clear who is telling the truth," the 49-year-old said.
FIGHTING IN LEBANON
On the war's other main front in Lebanon, heavy fighting continued.
Netanyahu announced Friday that Israeli forces had advanced beyond a river that runs around 30 kilometres (20 miles) north of the Lebanon-Israel frontier.
Hizbollah also said its forces launched attacks on northern Israel as well as Israeli troops trying to advance near the medieval Beaufort fortress, also known as Qalaat al-Chakif, a site Israel's forces had used as a base during their two-decade occupation of southern Lebanon ending in 2000.
A ceasefire between Israel and Hizbollah was supposed to have taken effect on April 17, but has never been observed with both sides accusing each other of violating the truce.
Lebanon was drawn into the war in early March when Teheran-backed Hizbollah launched rockets at Israel over the killing of Iran's supreme leader in US-Israeli attacks, prompting Israeli strikes and a ground invasion.
Israel and Lebanon began direct talks in April, with a fourth round expected next week in Washington following the meeting of military delegations from both sides on Friday. - AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Derailed train cars cleared as Rapid KL works to restore normal services
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Four train cars that derailed recently near the Chan Sow Lin station have been successfully rerailed and returned to the depot for technical inspection.
Rapid KL, in a Facebook post today, said recovery works were carried out yesterday and early this morning, involving the coupling of the train cars with a specialised track vehicle to enable their safe removal.
"Efforts are being intensified to ensure safe and comfortable train services for passengers can be restored as soon as possible," the post said.
On Thursday, a train on the LRT Ampang/Sri Petaling Line
derailed
near the station following a fault in a track switch, disrupting service schedules.
Transport Minister Anthony Loke yesterday said the affected track was expected to resume operations no later than next Wednesday.
He said all stations along the Ampang/Sri Petaling Line
remained operational
, although services were running on a modified schedule to ensure train movements could continue throughout the recovery period.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police verify Perlis MB's firearm licence, seize shotgun
Article:
KANGAR: Police have confirmed that Perlis Menteri Besar Abu Bakar Hamzah holds a valid licence for the shotgun used to shoot a rampaging cow on Thursday.
District police chief Assistant Commissioner Yusharifuddin Mohd Yusop said checks by the district police headquarters' licensing division confirmed it.
He added that police seized the shotgun and nine rounds of buckshot to assist in investigations.
"Police received a report from the public at 4.51pm yesterday regarding a viral 38-second video of the incident at Dataran Lok 9, Kuala Perlis.
"A police team from the Kangar district headquarters recorded a statement from the menteri besar.
"Based on his statement, the action to discharge the firearm was taken because the cow was rampaging and posing an immediate threat to workers and the public at the Kuala Perlis Premier Korban Programme," he said in a statement today.
Yusharifuddin added that the case is being investigated under Section 39 of the Arms Act 1960 for discharging a firearm in a public place.
The offence carries a maximum penalty of one year in jail, a fine not exceeding RM2,000, or both, upon conviction.
Those with information can contact investigating officer Assistant Superintendent Tengku Mohd Farid Tengku Ibrahim at 019-9939200 or 04-9082222, or the nearest police station.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sustained, intelligence-led crackdown needed to dismantle underground vape market
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Enforcement authorities must adopt sustained, intelligence-led operations to dismantle the underground market for drug-laced vape products and halt vaping among schoolchildren.
Alliance for a Safe Community chairman Tan Sri Lee Lam Thye said sporadic enforcement alone is insufficient to tackle the black market trade, which spans covert physical networks and online sales.
"This underground vape market cannot be addressed through sporadic raids alone.
"It requires sustained enforcement, stronger intelligence gathering, stricter penalties, tighter control of online sales, and close cooperation among various enforcement agencies," he said.
Lee said while some illicit supply chains operate off the radar, others were still accessible through vape outlets, where a wide variety of products are openly sold.
"Underground operators are particularly difficult to detect as they are not visible and often operate through online channels, making enforcement more complex.
"This is more difficult to handle because they are not visible, they cannot be seen, and they can do a lot of online sales underground," he said.
Lee said intelligence gathering was the most important element in addressing the issue, as it would allow authorities to identify hotspots and networks before action is taken.
"This is where authorities have to focus on intelligence gathering. With intelligence gathering, then they will be able to more or less identify which are the spots, and then follow up with action," he said.
He also called for enforcement efforts to be strengthened around schools, as well as the provision of convenient reporting channels for vape-related activities.
"Enforcement around schools is needed because, around schools, sometimes there are shops selling vapes, and also provide access to the public to report illegal vape activities," he said.
Lee added that the situation had reached alarming levels, with nearly 20,000 student-related vaping cases recorded nationwide in 2024.
He warned that vaping had evolved from a nicotine issue into a broader public health threat, particularly with the emergence of drug-laced vape products.
"Vaping is no longer merely a nicotine-related issue. It is increasingly becoming a gateway to substance abuse among young Malaysians," he said.
Lee said the popularity of vaping among students was driven by attractive packaging, appealing flavours, aggressive online marketing, peer influence, easy accessibility and the misconception that vaping was harmless compared to conventional smoking.
"Many young people were unaware that nicotine addiction could develop rapidly and have long-term effects on brain development, concentration, mental health and academic performance.
"Drug-laced vape products posed an even greater risk, as they were harder for parents and teachers to detect and could lead to addiction, behavioural problems, impaired judgment and serious health complications.
As such, Lee said the issue must be curbed urgently for several reasons, including protecting the health and well-being of young people, preventing early addiction, safeguarding the school environment, stopping students from being drawn into drug distribution networks and avoiding a future public health burden.
He also warned that some students could be exploited by syndicates involved in illicit vape distribution.
Lee said among the measures he proposed were intensified enforcement against sales and promotion to minors, regular raids on physical premises and online platforms, heavier penalties on suppliers, and stronger inter-agency cooperation involving schools, parents, police, health authorities, customs and local authorities.
He also called for awareness and prevention programmes in schools, training for teachers, counsellors and parents to detect early signs of vaping and substance use, enhanced monitoring of online sales channels and stricter regulation of vape flavours, packaging, advertising and product displays that appeal to minors.
Lee said the fight against vaping among students could not be left to enforcement agencies alone and required a whole-of-society approach involving families, communities, healthcare professionals, educational institutions and government agencies.
"Our children are among the nation's most valuable assets. Allowing vaping, particularly drug-laced vaping, to spread unchecked risks creating a generation burdened by addiction, health problems and social challenges," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Anwar: Sabah interim special grant increased to RM1.5 billion
Article:
PENAMPANG: The interim special grant for Sabah has been increased to RM1.5 billion this year from RM600 million previously, said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
He said the increase is a temporary measure pending a final mechanism for Sabah's 40 per cent revenue entitlement under the Malaysia Agreement 1963 (MA63).
"The actual figure for the 40 per cent revenue entitlement still needs to be finalised through negotiations between the federal and state governments," he added.
"On the 40 per cent revenue issue, although it is said to fall under financial arrangement 112D, in my view, it is settled. Do not dispute our decision to fulfil this promise," he said.
"Whether it is implemented in June or December must be decided by both committees and then brought to the state government at the highest level, followed by negotiations between the federal and state governments," he added.
He said this when officiating at the 2026 Sabah State-level Kaamatan Festival at Hongkod Koisaan, Kadazandusun Cultural Association (KDCA) here today.
Also present were Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor, Deputy Chief Minister I Datuk Seri Dr Joachim Gunsalam, who is also chairman of the 2026 Sabah State-level Kaamatan Festival Main Organising Committee, and KDCA president Tan Sri Joseph Pairin Kitingan.
Anwar said the commitment regarding the 40 per cent revenue payment has been brought to the Cabinet and officially announced in Parliament, but the current challenge lies mainly in its implementation mechanism.
The prime minister said he has tasked Second Finance Minister Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan and Treasury secretary-general Datuk Johan Mahmood Merican to refine negotiations with the Sabah state government.
He added that the negotiations must be carried out carefully, as any decision involving MA63 has direct implications for Sabah, Sarawak, and the states in Peninsular Malaysia.
"This decision is linked to the 1963 agreement (MA63). Therefore, every decision and its figures must be examined in detail. It takes some time, but it does not mean that the funds will not be provided," he said.
Countering claims that Sabah continues to be neglected, Anwar said the federal government collects around RM10 billion annually in revenue from Sabah, while development expenditure channelled back to the state amounts to a higher figure of RM17 billion.
The prime minister said the federal government also expects subsidies for petrol, diesel and liquefied petroleum gas in Sabah to rise to RM3 billion this year, compared with RM1 billion last year, following the ongoing global supply crisis.
He stressed that the disclosure of these figures is not intended to deny Sabah's 40 per cent revenue entitlement, but rather to provide a clearer picture to the public so they are not influenced by irresponsible political sentiment.
"Even amid soaring costs, the Madani Government remains committed to maintaining fuel prices, particularly RON95 petrol, at the lowest levels in the world to safeguard the welfare and livelihoods of the people.
"Do not deny (the federal government's efforts) and give the impression that we are being mistreated, treated as a stepchild, or made to suffer. This is petty politics. It will not foster unity. It is politics of misinformation, merely to seek support from certain ethnic groups, not based on facts," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Missing man sparks SAR operation, found asleep by washing machine next door
Article:
PADAWAN: A man who triggered a search and rescue operation after disappearing from a wedding reception in Kampung Sira here early today was found asleep next to a neighbour's washing machine.
A Sarawak Fire and Rescue Department operations centre spokesman said a distress call was received at 4.39am, prompting the deployment of a team from the Siburan fire station.
Two personnel in a Toyota Hilux pickup truck arrived at the scene at 5.14am to initiate the search alongside police officers, family members, and local villagers.
"Initial investigations revealed that the man, who is the brother-in-law of the complainant, had attended a wedding reception at a church in the village last night.
"After eating and drinking at the reception, he reportedly went outside to relieve himself but failed to return," he said.
Following a coordinated search effort, villagers discovered the man unhurt at 6am sleeping beside the household appliance next door.
The spokesman added that the operation was officially called off at 6.09am after medical checks confirmed the man sustained no injuries during his misadventure.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Civil society groups seek stronger checks in AG-PP separation bill
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Civil society groups have called on the government to ensure that proposed constitutional amendments separating the roles of the Attorney-General (AG) and Public Prosecutor (PP) result in an independent, accountable and credible prosecution service.
In a joint statement today, the groups urged the cabinet and members of parliament to closely scrutinise the proposed amendment and any recommendations made by the Parliamentary Special Select Committee (PSSC) before the legislation is debated and voted on in the Dewan Rakyat later this month.
The groups — Coalition for Clean and Fair Elections, Institute for Democracy and Economic Affairs, Rasuah Busters, Projek Sama and the Centre to Combat Corruption and Cronyism — warned that shortcomings in the proposed amendments could undermine the intended reform and create unintended constitutional consequences.
They said that while the bill was referred to the PSSC in March for further deliberation, neither the committee's report nor its proposed amendments had been made public.
The groups added that MPs must be given adequate time to review the committee's findings before being asked to debate or vote on constitutional changes of such significance.
"The integrity of the parliamentary process demands that MPs have full access to and sufficient time to review the PSSC's report and the proposed amendments before being asked to debate and vote on them.
"It is untenable for Parliament to enact constitutional amendments of this significance without MPs having had a genuine and meaningful opportunity to study the relevant materials," the statement said.
They called on the PSSC to immediately provide its report and proposed amendments to all MPs.
Should procedural rules prevent early disclosure, the groups said debate on the bill should be deferred until lawmakers and the public have had sufficient time to study the documents.
The civil society organisations also argued that the proposed reforms must include meaningful safeguards to ensure accountability in the appointment of the Public Prosecutor.
They said an independent prosecution service could only be achieved if the appointment process was transparent, accountable and protected from undue influence.
Among the measures proposed were requiring His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, to appoint the Public Prosecutor based on the advice of an independent body such as the Judicial and Legal Service Commission (JLSC), granting Parliament a substantive role in the appointment process, and publicly disclosing the names of shortlisted candidates.
The groups cautioned that placing sole responsibility for appointing the Public Prosecutor on the King could expose the constitutional monarchy to criticism if an appointee later proved unsuitable or abused the powers of the office.
"To safeguard the institution of the constitutional monarchy and ensure genuine accountability, the appointment process must clearly define responsibility and oversight."
The groups also urged the government to commit to introducing an enabling law to operationalise the constitutional amendments, including provisions governing the appointment process, parliamentary oversight, prosecutorial functions and ethical guidelines.
They said constitutional amendments alone would be insufficient without detailed legislation establishing how the new framework would function in practice.
In addition, the groups called for the Public Prosecutor's annual report to be tabled and debated in Parliament as part of efforts to strengthen accountability and transparency.
They urged the cabinet, which is scheduled to meet on June 5, and MPs debating the bill during the upcoming Dewan Rakyat sitting, to treat the reform as a matter of lasting constitutional significance.
"A constitutional reform that is rushed and inadequately deliberated may bring about grave unintended consequences and risks being worse than no reform at all."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar congratulates Tabung Haji, Amal on Saudi recognition
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has congratulated Lembaga Tabung Haji (TH) for receiving prestigious recognition from the Saudi Arabian Ministry of Hajj and Umrah.
This, Anwar said, reflects Malaysia's excellence in the management and welfare of pilgrims.
"This success reflects the commitment, professionalism and dedication of all TH staff and hajj officials who always strive to provide the best service in managing Malaysian pilgrims," he said in a social media post today.
Meanwhile, Anwar also extended congratulations to Amal by Malaysia Airlines, which received the "Misk Al-Khitam" award in recognition of its excellence in managing arrival procedures for pilgrims during this year's Hajj season.
He said the recognition demonstrates the capability and efficiency of Malaysian institutions and companies at the international level.
"May this success serve as a catalyst to further strengthen service quality and continue elevating the country's name in serving the guests of Allah," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Family of five escapes unhurt as suspected 'tekong darat' crashes car into house
Article:
TUMPAT: A family of five, including a 79-year-old grandmother and her two grandchildren, escaped unhurt this morning when a car driven by a suspected human trafficking agent crashed into their home.
The incident occurred at Kampung Tok Malik near Wakaf Bharu at 7am.
House owner Aminah Seman said she was reciting the Quran in her bedroom when she heard a loud noise outside the house.
"My daughter and I looked through the window and saw several men chasing another man. I immediately woke my grandchildren and we went outside to see what had happened," she said at her home.
At the time, her two grandchildren, aged 22 and 25, were asleep in the living room while her daughter and son-in-law were in another room.
The mother of nine was shocked to find that a car had crashed into their parked car before hitting the side of the house.
The impact damaged the vehicle and cracked the house structure.
"I am very grateful that my daughter, son-in-law, grandchildren, and I were not hurt.
"It is usually my routine to go outside every morning for exercise, but today I decided to stay indoors and recite the Quran instead. If I had been outside at the time, the situation could have been much worse," she said.
Aminah added that she later saw police detaining the suspect who was driving the vehicle.
Her son, Che Abdul Muiz Ibrahim, 38, said he was in Tanah Merah when he was informed of the incident.
"I rushed back to Wakaf Bharu immediately. I am thankful that my mother, sister, and the children are safe despite the frightening incident," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Kazakhstan offers to take Iran's uranium stockpile, IAEA chief tells FT
Article:
LONDON: Kazakhstan has offered to take Iran's uranium stockpile if the United States and Iran reach an accord on Teheran's contested nuclear programme, UN nuclear watchdog chief Rafael Grossi told the Financial Times on Friday.
The head of the International Atomic Energy Agency met with Kazakhstan's President Kassym-Jomart Tokayev in Astana this week.
The Financial Times said the Kazakh leader had expressed his country's "openness" to store the stockpile enriched to near weapons grade level.
The estimated 440 kilogrammes of uranium processed to 60 percent purity is at the centre of talks between the United States and Iran on extending the ceasefire in the war unleashed by US-Israel attacks.
US President Donald Trump has insisted that Iran must accept that it will not have a nuclear weapon and that the uranium is destroyed. Iran has insisted on its right to maintain a nuclear programme.
According to US news site Axios, a deal being discussed does not solve the uranium dispute, but would include an Iranian commitment not to build a nuclear bomb. - AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Govt steps up supply chain protection as job losses jump 21%
Article:
JOHOR BARU: The government will continue strengthening cooperation with various countries to safeguard supply chains, ensuring economic growth and protecting employment opportunities amid global economic uncertainty.
Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said the government's immediate priority is to keep the economy moving despite ongoing global challenges and disruptions to supply chains.
He said that during the early stages of an economic crisis, particularly within the first two to three months, the government's focus is on addressing supply-related issues that could disrupt business operations and affect jobs.
"The government's focus now is to ensure the economy continues to move so that jobs can be retained. In the first two to three months of a crisis, among the main issues to be tackled are those related to supply.
"This is why the government has taken steps to ensure the country's oil supply remains sufficient, in line with assurances that our oil supply is secure until the end of July," he told reporters after attending the Johor Baru parliamentary constituency's Perdana Aidiladha korban ceremony at Masjid Jamek Bandar Baru Uda here today.
He was responding to questions on measures being taken to address employment and unemployment concerns arising from global economic uncertainty.
Akmal said the government was not only engaging local industry players but also strengthening cooperation and discussions with various countries to secure strategic supply chains.
He said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim's upcoming visit to Japan would be used to discuss several supply-related issues identified by the ministry.
"The same matter was discussed during the recent visit by the Australian prime minister to Malaysia as part of efforts to strengthen supply guarantees and bilateral economic cooperation," he said.
Akmal added that the government had put in place various worker protection measures, including allocations under the Employment Insurance System (EIS), to assist workers should the impact of the economic downturn begin to be felt.
Recent reports stated that the government would take immediate measures to address the rising number of job losses recorded since April.
Anwar said the issue had been discussed at a National Economic Action Council (MTEN) meeting, with efforts focused on improving the situation and providing alternative employment opportunities, particularly for youths.
A total of 7,057 workers lost their jobs in April, a 21 per cent increase from 5,855 cases recorded in March.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Cabinet members extend Kaamatan greetings, highlight unity and heritage
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Cabinet members today extended Kaamatan greetings to the Kadazan-Dusun, Murut, and Rungus communities in Sabah, describing the festival as a symbol of unity and cultural heritage that underpins Malaysia's multiracial harmony.
Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali said Kaamatan is a valuable cultural legacy that serves as a source of strength for Sabahans.
He said this year's theme, Menuai Perpaduan, Meraikan Keharmonian, reflects the aspiration to preserve mutual respect, appreciate cultural diversity, and strengthen bonds across various backgrounds.
"May this year's Kaamatan celebration bring joy and renewed spirit for us to work together in building a more progressive, prosperous, and harmonious Sabah. Kotobian Tadau Tagazo do Kaamatan," he said.
Tourism, Arts and Culture Minister Datuk Seri Tiong King Sing said the festival showcases Malaysia's unique cultural heritage to the world, in line with the Visit Malaysia 2026 aspirations.
"May this year's Kaamatan Festival be celebrated with joy, gratitude, and harmony, further strengthening Malaysia's identity as a nation rich in culture, tradition, and unity," he said.
Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Minister Datuk Seri Arthur Joseph Kurup said the spirit of Kaamatan is closely linked to a shared responsibility in preserving the environment.
The Pensiangan member of parliament said traditional communities have long valued land and nature for survival, and future generations must be able to reap those benefits.
"Environmental sustainability is a shared responsibility, and harmony among people becomes more meaningful when we also live in harmony with our surroundings," he said.
Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail expressed hope that the celebration would reinforce peace, prosperity, and well-being for the nation.
Works Minister Datuk Seri Alexander Nanta Linggi reminded those travelling back to their hometowns to drive safely.
Also extending their greetings were Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said, Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek, Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abdul Kadir, and Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Rafizi's right to choose his own path, says PKR's Akmal
Article:
JOHOR BARU: PKR central leadership council member Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said former PKR deputy president Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli is free to join any political party as part of the democratic process.
Akmal, who is also the Johor Baru member of parliament, said every individual has the right to choose a political platform that aligns with their aspirations.
"I am confident that everyone has the space to be involved in any political party according to what suits their chosen path.
"This shows our commitment to continue strengthening a more mature political culture," he said when asked about the establishment of Bersama by Rafizi and former PKR vice-president Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad.
Asked whether he would leave PKR following Rafizi's move, Akmal declined to comment.
Akmal was previously regarded as one of the PKR leaders aligned with Rafizi's camp.
He lost the contest for the Johor Baru PKR chief's post last year but retained his position in the party's central leadership council as an elected member.
On May 17, Rafizi and Nik Nazmi resigned from PKR and vacated their respective parliamentary seats.
The duo subsequently took over Bersama, a political party founded by Tan Gin Theam and registered in George Town, Penang, on Dec 2, 2016.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Driver cheats death as runaway trailer plunges into ravine
Article:
GUA MUSANG: A 30-year-old trailer driver escaped unhurt yesterday when his vehicle rolled forward, crashed through a guardrail, and plunged into a ravine at Km90 of Jalan Gua Musang-Lojing.
The incident occurred at 7.30am after the driver parked the vehicle on the roadside, believing it was experiencing brake failure.
Shortly after he stepped out, the trailer unexpectedly moved forward.
District police chief Superintendent Sik Choon Foo said police received a report on the incident at 10am.
"Initial investigations revealed that the trailer was travelling from Port Klang, Selangor, to Lojing.
"The trailer driver did not sustain any injuries," he said today.
He added that further investigations are ongoing and urged those with information to contact the police.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: No plans to call off search for missing hiker, says Fire and Rescue DG
Article:
CYBERJAYA: The search and rescue (SAR) operation for missing hiker Jaslinda Saludin will continue, with no plans to suspend the search.
Fire and Rescue Department director-general Datuk Seri Nor Hisham Mohammad said about 50 personnel from various agencies remain deployed to sustain search operations and facilitate the continuous rotation of teams.
He said the decision to continue was based on the rescue team's experience and the area's conditions.
"Mountain search operations differ significantly from maritime searches."
"We will not stop. The search is ongoing and will continue," he said after attending the Korban Kasih programme at the Cyberjaya Fire and Rescue station today.
The programme was organised by the Sepang Parliamentary Service Centre in collaboration with the Housing and Local Government Ministry and the department.
Jaslinda, 49, went missing while climbing Gunung Batu Putih last Saturday.
The operation has entered a critical phase as she has been missing for more than 72 hours.
He added that 36 personnel from the department and police had been deployed to the Kem Sukaneka and Gunung Rayu sectors.
The operation is being supported by the General Operations Force, Forestry Department personnel and mountain guides to widen the search area.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Massive morning fire guts hardware, furniture factories in Sungai Buloh
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A hardware factory and a furniture factory were heavily damaged in a fire at the Kampung Baru Sungai Buloh light industrial area this morning.
Selangor Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Ahmad Mukhlis Mukhtar said an emergency call was received at 7.32am. Firefighters from the Sungai Buloh Fire and Rescue station were dispatched to the scene.
"Firefighters arrived eight minutes later and found the blaze involving two Class A factory buildings.
"The first factory, a 50ft-by-200ft hardware factory, was 90 per cent destroyed.
"The second, a furniture factory of the same size, sustained about 60 per cent damage," he said in a statement.
Mukhlis said no casualties were reported.
He added that personnel from the Bukit Jelutong, Subang Jaya, Damansara and Selayang fire stations were deployed to assist in the operation.
"A total of 26 firefighters, supported by four fire engines, two water tankers and two Emergency Medical Rescue Services (EMRS) vehicles, were involved in the firefighting operation," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Adults must take collective responsibility for children's online safety
Article:
CYBERJAYA: Online Safety Committee (OSC) chairman Tan Sri Hasnah Mohammed Hashim has urged all adults entrusted with the care of children to take collective responsibility for their online safety and well-being.
She said this duty extends beyond parents to teachers, babysitters, relatives, and the wider community.
"All adults who are taking care of children must be responsible, babysitters, parents, aunties and uncles as well.
"All Malaysians have to be responsible when it comes to children, to make sure that their interests are taken care of and not taken advantage of, and that their safety, physically and mentally, is looked after," she said in a recent interview.
Hasnah stressed the need for greater public awareness to protect children from online exploitation, abuse, harmful content, neglect, and mistreatment.
With children becoming digitally savvy at a younger age, she said parents and legal guardians must play a more active role in monitoring their online activities.
"You have a child of nine who knows better than a teenager. So, with all the risks out there on social media, parents or legal guardians have to monitor their children more closely and be more aware of what they are looking at," she said.
She warned that seemingly harmless online material could expose children to predators, scams, and inappropriate content disguised as child-friendly media.
Hasnah also expressed concern over parents oversharing photographs and videos of their children on social media, noting that many may not understand the long-term risks.
She said the committee would continue to study international approaches while ensuring that any recommendations remain suitable for Malaysia's social and legal environment.
The OSC, which acts as a neutral body under the Online Safety Act 2025 (Onsa), will recommend measures and guidelines to the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) to improve online safety and platform accountability.
"We are a neutral entity to ensure that providers are responsible and to provide guidelines for users in the future," she said.
Hasnah added that efforts to raise awareness should move beyond conventional printed materials to focus on visual and digital approaches, including podcasts and videos.
"There should be more podcasts about child awareness because to understand a certain subject matter, you also have to watch and listen. So, I think many people do not understand the risks that exist," she said.
Onsa, which came into effect on Jan 1, 2026, aims to create a safer Internet environment by requiring online platform providers to remove harmful content and respond swiftly to threats.
Following this, two new codes under the act, the Child Protection Code and the Risk Mitigation Code, will take effect on June 1, requiring digital platforms to take stricter action against harmful content and better protect children online.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Snap election looming? 'Mysterious' one-day Johor assembly sitting sparks speculation
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Speculation over an early Johor state election intensified yesterday after notices were circulated calling assemblymen to attend a special state legislative assembly sitting on June 22.
The development has reignited political chatter across the state, with leaders from both sides of the political divide increasingly convinced that Johor could be heading for another snap election within months.
Several assemblymen confirmed receiving invitations to attend the one-day sitting, fuelling expectations that the state assembly could be dissolved soon after.
While constitutional experts note that a dissolution does not require a special sitting and ultimately rests on the consent of the Sultan of Johor, political observers believe the latest development has placed the spotlight firmly on Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi, who is also Johor Barisan Nasional chairman.
The latest speculation comes amid growing strains between BN and Pakatan Harapan following
Onn Hafiz's declaration last month
that Johor BN intends to contest all 56 state seats in the next election.
The announcement, made a day before PH's convention at the Persada International Convention Centre, sent shockwaves through the unity government coalition and dominated political discussions throughout the gathering.
Several PH leaders openly criticised the move, arguing that it ran counter to the spirit of cooperation underpinning the federal unity government. Chants of "pengkhianat" (traitor) were also heard during parts of the convention.
Johor BN leaders, however, have maintained that the coalition is merely seeking to defend the mandate it won in the 2022 state election, pointing out that the current administration was formed as a BN government rather than under a unity government arrangement.
The coalition's confidence stems from its commanding performance in the last state election, when it secured 40 of the state's 56 seats and a two-thirds majority.
Political observers caution, however, that the next election could present a more complex challenge.
Unlike in 2022, the coming contest is expected to be fought with a larger electorate, greater youth participation and a far more polarised political landscape.
Political parties have already begun mobilising their machinery, while online campaigning and cyber activity have intensified in recent weeks amid mounting election speculation.
Adding to the political intrigue is the emergence of the
phrase "Budak Ni" (this boy)
, which has gained traction in Johor political circles after Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim recently referred to unnamed political rivals as "little boys" during a speech.
What began as a passing remark has since evolved into a slogan of sorts in Johor's political conversation, surfacing increasingly as speculation over a state election gathers momentum.
For now, there has been no official indication that the state assembly will be dissolved.
But with parties openly positioning for battle and election rhetoric escalating across the political divide, many believe Johor has already entered campaign mode, even if the starting whistle has yet to be blown.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Fadillah: Kaamatan Festival a symbol of unity and harmony
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Kaamatan Festival celebrated by the Kadazan-Dusun, Murut, and Rungus communities in Sabah is a symbol of gratitude, unity, and togetherness in the state's multiracial society.
Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof, in a Facebook post, said the harvest festival, observed annually on May 30 and 31, reflects the harmony enjoyed by Malaysians regardless of race, religion, or cultural background.
Fadillah, who is also Energy Transition and Water Transformation Minister, said the 2026 Kaamatan Festival theme, Harvesting Unity, Celebrating Harmony, aptly captures the spirit of cooperation among Sabahans, particularly in facing global challenges such as economic uncertainty, rising living costs, and energy and supply chain disruptions.
"This spirit is the foundation of Malaysia's strength in overcoming challenges and ensuring the nation remains stable, competitive, and sustainable," he said.
"May this year's Kaamatan Festival bring blessings and well-being, while further strengthening unity and goodwill among Malaysians. Kotobian Tadau Tagazo do Kaamatan," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor Umno, BN warned against complacency ahead of potential snap election
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Johor Umno and Barisan Nasional (BN) must not be lulled into a false sense of security by their crushing 2022 state election victory, a political observer warned.
Fakhrul Anwar Ramli, an advocate and solicitor, said a larger, younger, and potentially more volatile electorate could dramatically reshape the next political contest in the state.
He noted that the landslide win four years ago was achieved under exceptional circumstances, including low voter turnout during the Covid-19 pandemic and a state election held separately from the general election.
"In the 2022 Johor election, voter turnout was only 55 per cent. Even then, Umno did not secure more than 50 per cent of the popular vote," he said.
Fakhrul said the political equation could change if the next state election runs concurrently with the general election, which could push voter turnout to 80 per cent and bring an additional 500,000 voters to the ballot box.
"That would create a completely different political battlefield," he said.
He added that voters under 50 now account for nearly 68 per cent of Johor's electorate, describing them as less attached to traditional party loyalties and political personalities.
Fakhrul said the 2022 victory was also driven by high public expectations of BN and the campaigning influence of former prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Razak, whose appeal remained strong among segments of the electorate.
However, he acknowledged that Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi continues to enjoy a favourable public perception as a hands-on leader.
"Many public issues have been addressed during his administration, while various assistance programmes and initiatives have benefited Johoreans," he said.
The remarks come amid intensifying political activity and growing speculation that the state could be heading towards a snap election.
The political temperature has risen noticeably in recent weeks, marked by increased cyber activity, sharper political messaging and heightened grassroots mobilisation across party lines.
The situation escalated after Johor BN chairman Onn Hafiz announced that BN would contest all 56 state seats on its own, a day before the Pakatan Harapan (PH) Convention 2026 was held at the Persada Johor International Convention Centre, here.
Since then, several anonymous social media accounts believed to be linked to political cybertrooper networks have surfaced online, targeting rival coalitions and attempting to shape public narratives ahead of a possible election.
The trend mirrors tactics seen during the 2022 Johor election and the 15th General Election, where coordinated online campaigns, political attacks and anonymous postings were deployed to influence voter sentiment.
Speculation over an early dissolution of the state assembly intensified following
invitations issued
to assemblymen for a one-day sitting on June 22.
Although the dissolution of the assembly does not constitutionally require a sitting and ultimately rests on the consent of the Sultan of Johor, the development has fuelled political chatter.
The
speculation
gained further momentum following the combative tone at the recent PH convention, where Prime Minister and PH chairman Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim signalled the coalition's readiness for a full-scale electoral battle should tensions within the unity government worsen.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: US praises Malaysia, Asean for Thailand-Cambodia observer mission
Article:
SINGAPORE: The United States has praised Malaysia's leadership in rapidly deploying an Asean observer team to the Thailand-Cambodia border, describing the initiative as a meaningful contribution to regional peace and security.
Speaking at the 23rd IISS Shangri-La Dialogue today, US Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth commended Malaysia and Asean for taking proactive steps to ease tensions and support diplomatic efforts, saying the mission demonstrated the region's growing commitment to maintaining stability through collective action.
"Nations in the region are stepping forward to preserve peace, reduce tensions and support diplomacy with credible capability.
"I commend Malaysia and Asean for their efforts in support of President Donald Trump's historic peace deal," he said during his address titled United States' Strategy for Peace in the Indo-Pacific.
In October last year, Thailand and Cambodia signed a peace agreement known as the KL Peace Accord on the sidelines of the 47th Asean Summit in Kuala Lumpur.
The accord was signed by Thai Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul and Cambodian Prime Minister Hun Manet, and witnessed by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim in his capacity as Asean chair, as well as Trump.
Hegseth also said that several US partners in Southeast Asia have intensified efforts to strengthen their defence capabilities.
He said Singapore had consistently punched above its weight by investing significantly in its armed forces while serving as a key hub for US logistics operations and rotational deployments.
Indonesia, meanwhile, has made substantial progress in modernising its military and expanding joint exercises with the US, enhancing interoperability and contributing to regional stability, he said.
"Thailand continues to deepen military cooperation with the US to enhance interoperability and develop necessary readiness.
"We are also seeing the same seriousness of purpose from Vietnam," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PM Anwar: Madani govt determined to strengthen development in Sabah, Sarawak
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Madani Government is committed to ensuring Sabah and Sarawak receive equitable and sustainable development benefits through educational opportunities, quality healthcare, and comprehensive infrastructure development, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today.
In a Facebook post in conjunction with the Kaamatan and Gawai festivals, he said the effort is also aimed at ensuring the people of both states enjoy a more dignified quality of life.
He said Kaamatan and Gawai are thanksgiving celebrations rooted in the traditions and heritage of the communities in Sabah and Sarawak, observed in a spirit of togetherness, consensus and appreciation for the blessings of a bountiful harvest.
"Amid the diversity of customs, languages and cultures that enrich our nation, these celebrations serve as a reminder that Malaysia's strength lies in its ability to preserve harmony within its plural society, strengthen the bonds of brotherhood and foster a culture of mutual respect," he said.
Anwar said the celebrations also reflect the importance of togetherness in reinforcing national harmony.
"'Kotobian Tadau Tagazo Do Kaamatan' and 'Gayu Guru Gerai Nyamai' to the people of Sabah and Sarawak. May these celebrations bring prosperity and blessings, while further strengthening the spirit of consensus and togetherness that underpins harmony in our beloved Malaysia," he added. — BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Canadian poison seller pleads guilty to aiding suicides, avoids murder trial
Article:
NEWMARKET, Canada: The Canadian man who sold poison to distressed people worldwide pleaded guilty on Friday to 14 counts of aiding suicide but avoided a murder trial, an outcome one victim's father branded "a disgrace."
Kenneth Law, a 60-year-old former chef, ran online forums that offered people advice on how to end their lives and made fatal substances available for purchase.
The details of Law's operation have caused widespread outrage since his arrest in 2023.
The list of 41 countries where Law sent poison included Australia, China, France and Brazil. He sold 330 packages to people in the United Kingdom.
Canadian prosecutors had charged him with 14 counts of murder and 14 counts of aiding suicide.
Prosectors on Friday told the court in Newmarket, north of Toronto, that they no longer believed they could secure murder convictions.
Law then stood in a semi-enclosed area reserved for defendants, flanked by his three lawyers, and said "I plead guilty" to aiding 14 suicides in Canada.
Jeshennia Bedoya-Lopez, who died in 2022 aged 18, was one of Law's victims.
Her father, Leonardo Bedoya, told reporters outside the court that he found her dead in her bedroom.
"She was my only daughter, my light, my life," he said.
Three years after Law's arrest, he called Friday's outcome "a disgrace."
"That man does not even face the victims. He always keeps his back turned," Bedoya said in Spanish.
Despite pleading to a lesser offense, experts say Law could still receive a sentence of 10-20 years imprisonment.
Sentencing will be determined at a separate hearing in September, when the court will hear victim impact statements.
SOUGHT OUT CLIENTS
After the guilty pleas were entered, prosecutors devoted several hours to reading an "agreed statement of facts," which detailed how Law shipped material for suicide across Canada and abroad, often for about $80.
Law proactively looked for customers, the statement of facts said.
He would appear on a suicide discussion forum under the pseudonym "Greenberg."
[[nid:1451367]]
When users would mention the meat preservative sodium nitrite as an option for suicide, Law would direct them to one of his sites where the powder was available in lethal concentrations.
Prosecutors also played a recording of a call between Law and a British journalist posing as an interested customer.
The reporter from the Times of London asked if Law's business is legal. Law offered explanations he could give to police if questioned, including that the product can help improve a swimmer's lung capacity.
Prosectors also recounted how people, after taking their own lives, were often found by family members with an open package of Law's sodium nitrite near their body.
'ANGRY'
David Parfett's son Thomas was 22 when he ended his life in 2021 with materials supplied by Law.
Now an advocate for more rigorous legislation to confront online spaces that guide people toward harm, Parfett told AFP that Canadian authorities were missing an opportunity to establish the gravity of Law's conduct.
"If (Law) hadn't been offering detailed instructions about how to take your own life, then the chances are my son would still be here. So again, for me, it's murder," Parfett said.
In the agreed statement of facts, Law's poison was tied to 79 deaths in Britain.
Britain's National Crime Agency confirmed in a statement that Law will not face additional prosecution, but that the British deaths will be considered during Canadian sentencing.
A joint statement by the NCA and Britain's prosecution service said the agencies had explained their decision to not prosecute Law "in detail to the victims and their families."
Parfett said in a statement: "I am angry, but I am not surprised."
He reiterated the families' rebuffed calls for a UK public inquiry. "If our own country will not put anyone on trial for these deaths, the very least it can do is hold a proper inquiry into how they were allowed to happen."
Dalhousie University law professor Robert Currie told AFP that Law's prosecutors were handcuffed by the fact that, under Canadian law, it's not clear if the same conduct can amount to both aiding suicide and murder.  - AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Kaamatan: Hajiji urges state to stay unified as 'one big family'
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: The annual Kaamatan Festival is more than a cultural tradition, serving as a symbol of gratitude, unity and harmony among Sabahans, Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor said.
He said this year's theme, "Menuai Perpaduan, Meraikan Keharmonian" (Harvesting Unity, Celebrating Harmony), aptly reflects Sabah's spirit as a state rich in cultural diversity, customs and beliefs.
Hajiji said Sabah's diverse heritage and cultural traditions remain a key strength that continues to unite its people as one family.
"Let us continue to preserve the cultural heritage passed down by our ancestors while strengthening unity and the spirit of togetherness for the progress of our beloved state," he said in a statement today.
He also extended Kaamatan greetings to all Sabahans.
"Kotobian Tadau Tagazo Do Kaamatan," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: U Mobile ULTRA5G: 390mil scam attempts successfully blocked since network inception
Article:
FOLLOWING its selection by the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) to provide Malaysia's second 5G network, U Mobile has focused on expanding its ULTRA5G infrastructure to support a more diverse telecommunications ecosystem.
Positioned as a next-generation network, it is designed to address both the data demands and security requirements of the current digital environment.
As digital activity increases, the network has been engineered to manage high-speed throughput while addressing the prevalence of cyber threats.
NETWORK-LEVEL SECURITY AND SCAM PREVENTION
A core feature of this infrastructure is Ultra Security, a built-in next-gen security function designed to protect users on the network against phishing SMS, fraudulent voice calls and flagged websites. This protection operates at the network level, meaning it does not require manual activation or the installation of third-party applications by the user.
Internal data indicates that this mobile security platform has intercepted over 390 million scam attempts since the network began operations.
By blocking these threats at the network layer, the system prevents many fraudulent communications from reaching the user's device.
CLASS-LEADING SPEED AND COVERAGE
In addition to its security features, ULTRA5G has established specific performance benchmarks. Based on Ookla Speedtest awards, the network has been recognised as having the fastest 5G speeds locally, which frequently reach between 500Mbps and 1000Mbps.
The network's technical architecture includes:
• Capacity management:
Doubled network capacity designed to manage traffic and reduce congestion in busy areas.
• Indoor signal strength:
Engineered for stronger indoor coverage, with data suggesting users are more likely to maintain a consistent 5G signal in environments such as shopping malls and transit hubs.
• Advanced integration:
Uses 64T64R Massive MIMO technology to support more simultaneous connections and peak performance in urban centres.
ENHANCED PROTECTION LAYER
For users seeking additional security layers, U Mobile recently launched Ultra Security Pro on April 14. This suite includes identity protection, device security and cyber insurance.
Subscribers on new Ultra Postpaid and Family plans are eligible for a three-month trial of these features. This follows the standard Ultra Security features already provided to all Ultra Prepaid and Postpaid subscribers at no additional cost.
PUBLIC ESIM TRIAL
To allow for direct evaluation of the network, U Mobile provides a seven-day ULTRA5G trial. The trial includes 10GB of 5G data and calls to all networks.
The trial is available to first-time customers with eSIM-compatible devices and can be activated via the MyUMobile App.
The service ends automatically after seven days without recurring charges, and users have the option to migrate to permanent Ultra plans to maintain connectivity.
More information regarding the ULTRA5G infrastructure and seven-day trial can be found
here
.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'Alarm' in Asia over China build-up, US seeks regional balance - Hegseth
Article:
SINGAPORE: There is "rightful alarm" in the Pacific over China's military build-up and the United States seeks a regional balance where no state has unchecked power, Pentagon chief Pete Hegseth said on Saturday.
"When we look across the region today, there is rightful alarm regarding China's historic military build-up and the expansion of its military activities in the region and beyond," Hegseth told Asia's premier defence summit in Singapore.
He added that Washington did not seek "needless confrontation in the region."
Hegseth was speaking at the Shangri-La Dialogue, which brings together top defence officials and experts from around 45 countries.
It has historically provided a setting for both open debate and behind-closed-doors diplomacy.
In his headline speech, Hegseth said Washington sought "a genuinely stable equilibrium (in Asia) that works for Americans as well as our allies."
That means "a favourable but durable balance of power in which no state, including China, can impose its hegemony and hold the security or prosperity of our nation and our allies in question", he added.
Hegseth is leading a bumper US delegation, in stark contrast to rival China, which has sent a panel of military experts and scholars instead of defence minister Dong Jun for the second year running. - AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Unity minister calls for wiser digital culture in Wesak Day message
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 30 — National Unity Minister Datuk Aaron Ago Dagang has called on Malaysians to uphold human values, strengthen unity and progress towards a more ethical, well-mannered and wise digital society.
In his Wesak Day greetings to Buddhists nationwide, he said the theme of this year’s national-level celebration, “
Mendepani Era Digital, Memupuk Kebijaksanaan Bersama
”, carries a deeply significant message for all Malaysians.
“In today’s digital world, unity is tested not only in the real world but also on social media. (While) a single post can unite society, a slander can destroy national harmony,” he said in a Facebook post today.
Aaron said the digital era poses challenges, including the spread of hatred, fake news, extremism and a culture of judging others without factual basis.
In this regard, he stressed that Madani values such as respect, compassion and social sustainability must be reflected in daily life, including in the use of social media.
“What we write, share and comment on reflects the maturity of our society. Every citizen has a responsibility to be an agent of unity, not one who spreads hatred.
“Malaysians need to be a mature digital society, to think before sharing, verify before believing and respect even when views differ,” he said.
Aaron said the Madani Government believes interfaith dialogue is a key foundation in strengthening national unity as it provides the best platform to build understanding, bridge gaps and foster respect among communities of different races and religions.
According to him, Malaysia was built on the strength of embracing and celebrating diversity, which forms the pillar of the nation’s harmony and stability.
Wesak Day, which will be celebrated tomorrow, is the most important holy day for Buddhists, commemorating the birth, enlightenment and death of Siddhartha Gautama, the founder of Buddhism. — Bernama

Title: Malaysia backs regional push for stronger military cooperation, joint drills at Shangri-La Dialogue in Singapore
Article:
SINGAPORE, May 30— Asean defence ministers today agreed to step up joint military training and exercises, particularly in humanitarian assistance and disaster relief operations.
During a breakfast meeting held on the sidelines of the 23rd Shangri-La Dialogue, the ministers also reaffirmed the importance of Asean centrality and the bloc’s role in promoting regional peace, stability and prosperity.
According to Singapore’s Defence Ministry (Mindef), the ministers exchanged views on expanding cooperation through initiatives such as Indonesia’s Exercise Trident Resolve, scheduled for September 2026 and involving Asean Defence Ministers’ Meeting Plus (ADMM-Plus) countries, as well as enhanced information-sharing through platforms such as the Republic of Singapore Navy’s Information Fusion Centre.
“The ministers agreed to redouble efforts to cooperate through the ADMM and the ADMM-Plus,” the ministry said in a statement.
The meeting was co-hosted by Singapore Defence Minister Chan Chun Sing and Philippines Secretary of National Defence Gilberto Teodoro Jr, and attended by defence leaders and representatives from Asean member states. Among those attending was Malaysian Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin.
The ministers further reaffirmed the importance of upholding international law and norms, particularly the right of transit passage under the United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea (Unclos).
They also stressed the need to keep international airways and waterways open, secure and safe for the free flow of trade and supplies, including through the Straits of Malacca and Singapore, Mindef said. — Bernama

Title: PM Anwar’s Aidiladha contribution benefits 2,000 Orang Asli residents in Ulu Kinta
Article:
IPOH, May 30 — About 2,000 residents from seven Orang Asli villages in Ulu Kinta benefited from an Aidiladha qurban programme following the contribution of eight cows by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
Tambun parliamentary special officer Mohamad Hairul Amir Sabri said the programme was organised by the Tambun Parliamentary Community Service Centre in collaboration with the Department of Orang Asli Development (JAKOA), with the participation of the Orang Asli community.
He said the initiative was part of ongoing efforts to ensure the Orang Asli community in the parliamentary constituency could also share in the Aidiladha celebration and benefit from the distribution of sacrificial meat.
The programme involved Orang Asli community from Kampung Makmur, Kampung Tonggang, Kampung Chadak, Kampung Suluh, Kampung Suak Petai, Kampung Choh and Kampung Baduk.
“The response has been very encouraging compared with previous years, so this year we expanded the programme and increased community participation,” he told reporters after the Aidiladha Qurban Perdana and Kenduri Rakyat 2026 programme at Kampung Makmur here today.
Mohamad Hairul Amir said the Prime Minister had contributed about 170 cows for distribution throughout the Tambun parliamentary constituency in conjunction with Aidiladha this year.
He said the contribution enabled mosques, surau, residents’ associations and local communities to carry out
qurban
activities and distribute meat to those in need.
“This reflects the Prime Minister’s continued commitment to ensuring the welfare of the people remains a priority regardless of background,” he said.
Meanwhile, Kampung Makmur Tok Batin Jailani Rasid said the programme benefited the Orang Asli community while strengthening ties among residents.
“We are grateful for the contribution of sacrificial cows and meat. Programmes like this provide an opportunity for residents from different villages to come together and participate in the
qurban
ritual,” he said. — Bernama

Title: Public Service Dept warns action may be taken against those spreading false info about govt agencies
Article:
KAMPAR, May 30 — Individuals found spreading videos or false information that tarnish the image of government ministries or agencies may face action.
Public Service Department (PSD) deputy director-general (Operations) Datuk Mohd Shaiful Ibrahim said such action was necessary to protect the credibility of government departments, as false information could undermine public confidence in the civil service.
He said departments have the right to defend their reputation if false information is circulated and damages their image, adding that police reports can be lodged to identify those responsible for spreading such content.
He said cases warranting further action would be referred to the Attorney General’s Chambers for prosecution, stressing that every case would first undergo a thorough investigation.
“If no offence is found, those involved will be cleared. Otherwise, further action will be taken in accordance with departmental procedures,” Mohd Shaiful told reporters after officiating the department’s Madani Adopted Village Korban programme at Kampung Kota Bharu here today.
On the programme, Mohd Shaiful said the PSD contributed five cows for the korban (sacrificial ritual), which involved about 100 participants comprising PSD staff, personnel from the Kampar District and Land Office, and locals.
He said the sacrificial meat was distributed to more than 300 recipients from the local community.
“We started at about 7am and the entire process, from slaughtering and preparing the meat to cooking and distribution, was carried out jointly by PSD staff, the Kampar District and Land Office, and villagers.
“The spirit of cooperation and togetherness reflected through this korban programme is also in line with Madani values that emphasise compassion, respect and community well-being,” he said. — Bernama

Title: Kedah Domestic Trade Ministry probes three fleet card misuse cases
Article:
ALOR SETAR, May 30 — The Kedah Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living (KPDN) is investigating three cases of fleet card misuse and reviewing companies granted the facility to prevent abuse of subsidised fuel.
Kedah KPDN chief enforcement officer Zalina Aziz said the ministry is also reassessing companies provided with fleet card facilities to ensure they are not misused or in breach of stipulated conditions.
“If any breach of conditions or misuse is detected, we will recommend the cancellation of the fleet card to headquarters,” she told Bernama.
On the implementation of Op Tiris Bersepadu 4.0 carried out in collaboration with Royal Malaysia Police and several other agencies, she said KPDN recorded 30 cases in the state, comprising 16 involving diesel seizures and 14 involving petrol.
“Op Tiris, which has been conducted since March 16, has recorded total seizures valued at RM1.4 million, with 26 vehicles also confiscated,” she said.
Zalina said Kedah KPDN currently has about 190 enforcement personnel across five branches statewide, but continued to face constraints in monitoring all 362 petrol stations in the state.
She said 30 petrol stations are under special monitoring, including 18 located in border areas such as Bukit Kayu Hitam, Durian Burung and Baling.
“In industrial areas such as Sungai Petani and Kulim, the challenge is greater due to the numerous entry and exit routes and vast factory areas, making fuel smuggling activities difficult to predict,” she said.
In addition, she said a number of them were found to have frequently changed vehicles and registration numbers to avoid detection by authorities, and carrying out their operations in the early hours of the morning when fewer workers are present at fuel stations.
“However, close cooperation with the PDRM has helped in terms of intelligence gathering and field operations, while also enhancing the safety of enforcement personnel during operations,” she added. — Bernama

Title: Police confirm Perlis MB’s shotgun legally licensed, seize weapon after viral sacrificial cow shooting
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 30 — Perlis Menteri Besar Abu Bakar Hamzah holds a valid firearms licence for the shotgun used to shoot a sacrificial cow that had reportedly turned aggressive, police said, after a video of the incident sparked debate online.
In a statement today, Kangar district police chief Assistant Commissioner Yusharifuddin Mohd Yusop said checks by the district police licensing division confirmed that the firearm was legally registered to Abu Bakar.
Police have nevertheless seized the shotgun and nine buckshot cartridges as part of an investigation into the incident, which took place during a korban programme in Kuala Perlis.
The investigation was launched after police received a report from a member of the public at 4.51pm yesterday regarding a 38-second video circulating on social media showing the incident at Dataran Lok 9.
Yusharifuddin said officers had recorded Abu Bakar’s statement.
“Based on his statement, the action (of firing the shot) was taken because the cow had become aggressive and was threatening workers and members of the public during the Kuala Perlis Prime Korban Programme,” he said.
